role_hints.role: Research staff — social scientists and research assistants
role_hints.oews_basis: 19-4061 Social Science Research Assistants 7.9% of wage bill + 19-3099 Social Scientists and Related Workers 5.0% + survey researchers/statisticians in the unlisted tail, est. ~8-12% more
role_hints.note: Literature reviews, survey instrument drafting, coding of qualitative data, statistical analysis and report writing are heavily AI-exposed; study design judgment, IRB navigation and sponsor-facing interpretation persist. Plausible within-role fraction 0.3-0.5.

role_hints.oews_basis: not in listed top-10; interviewers, enumerators and data-collection staff in the tail, est. ~8-12%
role_hints.note: Phone/web survey operations automate substantially (and are already shrinking); in-person interviewing, field enumeration and site visits persist where methodology requires them.

role_hints.role: CRM archaeology and field science staff
role_hints.oews_basis: 19-3091 Anthropologists and Archeologists 2.0% (2.8% of employment) — a genuine signature of cultural resource management firms in this code, plus field techs in the tail, est. ~3-5% more
role_hints.note: Section 106 surveys, shovel testing and excavation are statutory physical fieldwork; report writing around it automates.

role_hints.oews_basis: 21-1023 Mental Health and Substance Abuse Social Workers 1.5% + 21-1018 Substance Abuse/Behavioral Disorder Counselors 1.8% — an oddity in an 'R&D' code, signaling research organizations that also deliver/evaluate behavioral programs
role_hints.note: Human-delivered program services; documentation automates but the service is relational.

role_hints.oews_basis: 11-1021 General and Operations Managers 11.6% + 13-1082 Project Management Specialists 3.2% + 13-1199 Business Operations Specialists 3.1%
role_hints.note: Sponsor relationships (federal COs, foundations) and proposal leadership persist; contract administration, compliance reporting and internal coordination automate heavily.

role_hints.oews_basis: 43-9061 Office Clerks 2.8% + 43-6014 Secretaries/Admin Assistants 1.7% + admin tail est. ~6-8% more
role_hints.note: Data entry, document production, scheduling — directly automatable; note the unusually clerical-heavy mix vs other R&D codes.

role_hints.oews_basis: not in listed top-10; 54172 top-10 covers only ~40% of wage bill
role_hints.note: Statisticians/data staff are heavily AI-augmented; IT and facilities mixed — allocate explicitly.

source_hints.source: AAPOR (American Association for Public Opinion Research) conference and task-force output on AI in survey research
source_hints.why: The survey-research profession's own debate on AI interviewing and synthetic respondents — both adoption evidence and the terminal-value mechanism.

source_hints.source: Insights Association research and industry reports
source_hints.why: Covers the research industry's AI adoption; note its center of gravity is 541910 market research — check segment coverage before quoting.

source_hints.source: ACRA (American Cultural Resources Association) member surveys
source_hints.why: The CRM archaeology trade association — firm size, owner demographics and market data for the one clearly roll-up-shaped sub-segment; confirm current survey publications.

source_hints.source: Professional Services Council (PSC) federal market outlooks
source_hints.why: Federal services budget and contracting-structure evidence — most large-firm revenue here is cost-reimbursable federal work.

source_hints.source: Trade press coverage of CRM consolidation (e.g., SWCA and PE-backed environmental/CRM platforms)
source_hints.why: CRM archaeology has documented acquisition activity via environmental-services consolidators — for active_consolidators; verify names and pace.

capture_questions: Federal evaluation and survey contracts — the revenue core — are cost-reimbursable or T&M with negotiated (NICRA-audited) indirect rates: AI-driven hour reductions lower billings and pass savings to the government. CRM archaeology bills fixed-price per project. What is the dominant sub-segment's split across these mechanisms, and who keeps AI savings under each?
Who owns the client relationship — the firm's contract vehicles, IDIQs and past-performance record, or named principal investigators whose reputations win recompetes?

For CRM: agencies and developers must hire qualified archaeologists (Secretary of the Interior standards) for Section 106 compliance — does that credential requirement stop AI bypass, or can one credentialed principal stamp AI-drafted reports over minimal fieldwork?

What stops federal and foundation sponsors from insourcing analysis and evaluation with AI, buying only the physical data collection — and is the 2025-2026 federal contracting environment already forcing that shift?
terminal_value_question: Two distinct mechanisms decide terminal value here and the run must not blur them: (1) statutory fieldwork — NHPA Section 106 and NEPA compliance require physical archaeological survey by qualified professionals, demand AI cannot replace but deregulation could shrink; (2) sponsored research — federal/foundation-funded surveys and evaluations, where AI (and synthetic-respondent methods) can replace the product itself and where the 2025 wave of federal contract terminations (education/health evaluation contracts) already cut the demand base — verify its depth and persistence rather than assuming pre-2025 baselines. For the dominant sub-segment, which mechanism governs, and is AI cutting the operator's costs or dissolving what the sponsor pays for?
special_notes: Sub-segment heterogeneity: this code mixes (a) federal/foundation survey and program-evaluation contractors, (b) CRM archaeology firms (the anthropologists/archeologists in the record are the tell), and (c) research orgs delivering behavioral-health programs (the social workers/counselors in the record). These have different pricing, moats and buyers — identify the top-3 revenue sub-segments, score the DOMINANT one, and set heterogeneous: true if scores diverge. Hypothesis, flagged as hypothesis: federal evaluation/survey research is dominant by revenue; CRM archaeology is the most roll-up-shaped.

Nonprofit/employee-owned contamination: the industry's giants (RTI, NORC, Westat-type organizations) are nonprofit or employee-owned — they distort market-size and wage statistics and are not acquisition comps; the acquirable universe is the small for-profit tail.
Pricing-model deflation candidate: cost-reimbursable federal contracts with audited NICRA rates pass AI savings to the government — model in pi_dist explicitly for the federal slice.

Demand shock: 2025 federal cost-cutting terminated large numbers of evaluation and survey contracts (Dept. of Education/IES and HHS among them) — single-year and pre-2025 revenue baselines are unreliable in both directions; verify current federal RFP flow before scoring demand.
EBITDA normalization: n_band is only 258 and micro firms' profit is SDE with owner comp inside; CRM firms also carry heavy seasonal field-labor swings — normalize across seasons.

Boundary confusion: market research/polling is 541910 (not this code), environmental consulting doing NEPA work is 541620, and many CRM firms sit inside environmental/engineering firms coded 541620/541330 — association data (ACRA) spans firms across codes; check coverage.
